title: Samagra Shiksha
text_content: Samagra Shiksha Skip to main content Skip to navigation Screen Reader Access Text Size: A- A A+ A A Previous Next About Samagra Shiksha The Union Budget, 2018-19, has proposed to treat school education holistically without segmentation from pre-nursery to Class 12. Samagra Shiksha - an overarching programme for the school education sector extending from pre-school to class 12 has been, therefore, prepared with the broader goal of improving school effectiveness measured in terms of equal opportunities for schooling and equitable learning outcomes. It subsumes the three schemes of Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan (SSA), Rashtriya Madhyamik Shiksha Abhiyan (RMSA) and Teacher Education (TE). This sector-wide development programme/scheme would also help harmonise the implementation mechanisms and transaction costs at all levels, View More Major Features of Samagra Shiksha Updates Samagra Shiksha Scheme Guidelines for Provisioning of Academic Resource Person for Career Counselling in the BRCs under Samagra Shiksha School Nutrition (Kitchen) Gardens Guidelines More... Initiatives Support at Pre-School level (Early Childhood Care and Education) Support has been provided for the efforts of State Govt in providing Pre-school education in schools of up to 2 years duration.. Know More Padhe Bharat Badhe Bharat Padhe Bharat Badhe Bharat is being implemented under the aegis of the Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan in 2014 to ensure quality at the foundational years of schooling i.e., classes I & II. Know More Library grant for promoting reading habits In order to complement the activities under Padhe Bharat Badhe Bharat and inculcate the reading habits among students of all ages, strengthening of school libraries is being undertaken including provision of books. Know More Sports and Physical Education Sports in schools have immense benefits for both children and for educational systems. Sports have the potential to make distinctive contributions to the development of childrenâs fundamental movement skills and physical competences.. Know More Experiential Learning (Rangotsav) For the promotion of experiential learning and joyful learning, various activities are organized for both students and teachers. Some of the major activities under taken are Kala Utsav; Role Play Competition. Know More Inclusive Education for Children with Special Needs Support has been provided for various student oriented activities which include identification and assessment of Children with Special Needs (CWSN), provision of aids, appliances.. Know More Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalaya (KGBV) Bridging gender and social category gaps at all levels of school education is one of the major objectives of the Samagra Shiksha. Consequently, to encourage the participation of girls.. Know More Constitution of Youth Club and Eco Club Youth clubs in schools are an instrument to develop life skills, build self-esteem, develop self-confidence and resilience and counter negative emotions of stress, shame and fear. Know More Self Defence Training (RAKSHA) Keeping in view, the rising number of crimes against girls in the country, it is important to provide self-defence training to them in the schools to ensure their safety and security. Know More School Safety Children have right to live with dignity and have access to education in an environment that is safe protective and conducive to growth & development. Know More Vocational Education The scheme seeks to integrate vocational education with general academic education with an aim to prepare educated, employable and competitive human resource for various sectors of the economy and the global market. Know More Integrated Teacher Training Programme (Elementary level) In-service teacher and teacher educators training have been an integral part of erstwhile Schemes of SSA, RMSA and CSSTE. Know More School Based Assessment (SBA) Preparations for NAS 2020 (Pre NAS 2020) interventions have been initiated to reach out to all the districts of different States and UTs. Know More Swachh Vidyalaya The Swachh Vidyalaya Initiative started with the construction of toilets, then moved to the maintenance of toilets and activities such as hand washing .. Know More Shaala Siddhi The programme envisions reaching 1.6 million schools in the country through a compressive system of school evaluation. know more... NAS & SAS, Learning Outcomes To monitor improvement in childrens learning levels and to periodically assess the health of the government education system as a whole. know more... Training of Untrained Teachers (NIOS) The programme has been developed by the Academic Department, NIOS on the initiative of MHRD, Govt. of India, keeping in view the NCF 2005, RTE 2009 and NCFTE 2010. know more... School Leadership and Management National Centre for School Leadership has been established to empower school leaders with knowledge, skills and attitudes to transform schools. know more...
